data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-ff45858b">
    <title>graph navigation: click-to-navigate, viewport persistence, pan/zoom fix, staleness filter</title>
    <description>
Fix the document dependency graph interactivity. Addresses US-081 AC which is specced but unimplemented.

Bugs to fix:
1. Pan/zoom sizing race: D3Graph.svelte uses el.clientWidth at init time which may be 0. Replace with ResizeObserver: set up simulation once observer fires with non-zero dimensions. This also handles window resize.
2. Node click = no handler: nodeGroup has mouseenter/mousemove/mouseleave but no onclick. Add click handler that navigates to /nodes/:nodeId/projects/:projectId/documents/:path (for markdown artifacts) or /artifacts/:artifactId (for other types). Distinguish by mediaType from graph node data.

Viewport persistence:
- Save zoom transform (x, y, k) to component state or URL param (e.g. ?zoom=1.2&pan=120,45) on each zoom/pan end event.
- On Back navigation, restore the saved transform before the D3 effect runs.

New features:
- Filter chips on graph page: filter nodes by mediaType (markdown, image, unknown) and staleness (fresh, stale, missing). Hidden nodes removed from simulation; edges to hidden nodes removed.
- Node color = staleness: green=fresh, amber=stale, red=missing (not dependsOn.length as today).
- 'View document list' link on graph page → /documents (which redirects to /artifacts).
- 'View in Graph' link on artifact detail page (once ddx-d7d1119d exists) → /graph with node pre-selected.

IntegrityPanel path links: currently link to /documents/... — update to /artifacts/:artifactId once /artifacts route exists; keep /documents fallback until then.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Pan/zoom works on first render with ResizeObserver; resizing window re-layouts graph.
2. Clicking a graph node navigates to the document/artifact detail without a full page reload.
3. Pressing Back from a document opened via graph click returns to graph with the same zoom/pan position.
4. Filter chips filter visible nodes + edges; node colors reflect staleness (green/amber/red).
5. 'View document list' link on graph page navigates to /artifacts?mediaType=text%2Fmarkdown.
6. graph.spec.ts covers: initial render → pan/zoom → click node → verify navigation → Back → verify viewport restored → apply staleness filter → verify node count changes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:ui, area:frontend, feat-008, graph, bug</labels>
    <metadata spec-id="FEAT-008" base-rev="999565c94979c5d40e66cf8d90e8147ddf9b3baa" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260501T030628-7b39c14f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-008" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-web-ui.md">Feature: DDx Server Web UI</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, writing `.ddx/executions/20260501T030628-7b39c14f/no_changes_rationale.txt` is mandatory before you exit. Include: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. If you exit without a commit and without this file, DDx records `no_evidence_produced` and treats the attempt as a harness failure, not as legitimate no_changes. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: Commit succeeded. Here's the AC verification: **AC-1 — Pan/zoom with ResizeObserver:** `D3Graph.svelte` uses a `ResizeObserver` on the SVG element; `rebuild(el, width, height)` is called when dimensions are first non-zero, and again on resize. The old `el.clientWidth || 960` fallback is gone. **AC-2 — Click navigates without full reload:** `D3Graph.svelte` already had the click handler; `graph/+page.svelte`'s `handleNodeClick` calls `goto()` (SPA navigation) routing to `/documents/:path` for markdown or `/artifacts/:artifactId` for other types. **AC-3 — Back restores viewport:** `+page.ts` reads `?zoom=N&pan=X,Y` URL params into `initialTransform` and returns it as page data. D3Graph applies it and skips auto-fit. `handleTransformChange` in `+page.svelte` writes zoom/pan to URL via `history.replaceState` on each zoom/pan end event. **AC-4 — Filter chips + staleness colors:** Filter chips for mediaType (Markdown/Image/Unknown) and staleness (Fresh/Stale/Missing) in `+page.svelte`; `visibleNodes`/`visibleLinks` derived from active filters; node fill color in `nodeColorClass()` = green/amber/red by staleness. **AC-5 — "View document list" link:** `+page.svelte` header includes `View document list`. **AC-6 — graph.spec.ts coverage:** TC-039 (click node → `/documents/`), TC-040 (navigate with preset viewport URL params → click → Back → verify params preserved), TC-041 (stale filter chip → node count changes from 3 → 1).
